description_for_human: Analyze Copilot usage, seat data, and spend to surface ROI insights.
description_for_model: Use this plugin when the user provides pasted text, CSV-style content, spreadsheet exports, or document summaries containing Copilot usage, seats, spend, adoption, or ROI-related data. The plugin helps extract and structure the data for analysis and recommendation generation.

conversation_starters.title: Analyze a usage export
conversation_starters.text: Analyze this Copilot usage export and tell me whether the company is getting value from it.

functions.name: analyze_copilot_value
functions.description: Analyze Copilot usage, seats, and spend data from text or document extracts and return a structured value assessment.

source_text.description: Extracted text or spreadsheet contents containing usage, seats, and spend data.

company_name.description: Optional company name to label the analysis.

source_type.description: The source format of the input data.

returns.description: A structured analysis payload containing metrics, insights, risks, and recommendations.

reasoning.description: This function analyzes Copilot usage and spend data to help identify ROI and adoption patterns.
instructions: Use the provided source_text as the only source of business facts.
Extract seats, usage, spend, and time-based signals when present.
If the input is incomplete, return the missing fields instead of guessing.

responding.description: Return the analysis in a business-friendly structure that can be summarized by the agent.
instructions: Summarize the analysis as a scorecard, insights, gaps, and recommendations.
Do not invent metrics or conclusions that are not supported by the input.

functions.name: predict_copilot_value
functions.description: Run the trained ROI, waste, and expansion models on a company or department's Copilot numbers and return exact, reproducible figures plus a first-principles finance breakdown.

company_name.description: Optional company or department label shown on the ROI card.

licensed_users.description: Number of users assigned a Copilot license.

active_users.description: Number of users actively using Copilot.

adoption_rate.description: Active users divided by licensed users (0-1). Derived when omitted.

app_mix_score.description: Breadth-of-usage score across Word, Outlook, Teams, and Excel actions.

avg_hours_saved_per_user_month.description: Average hours saved per active user per month.

loaded_hourly_cost_usd.description: Blended loaded hourly labor cost (salary plus overhead).

license_cost_month_usd.description: Total monthly Copilot license spend in USD.

company_size.description: Total employee headcount of the company.

enablement_cost_month_usd.description: Optional monthly enablement/training cost in USD. Defaults to 0.

roi_percent_month.description: Optional actual monthly ROI %. When omitted, the ROI model prediction is chained into the expansion model.

returns.description: A JSON payload with roi, waste, expansion (probability and recommendation), and a first-principles finance_model breakdown.

reasoning.description: This function executes the trained regression and classification models to produce precise ROI, waste, and expansion numbers.
instructions: Map the user's data to the numeric inputs; derive adoption_rate from active and licensed users when it is not given.
Prefer this function when the user wants an exact, reproducible figure or asks to run the model.
If required numeric inputs are missing, ask for them instead of guessing.

responding.description: Return the model outputs so the agent can fold them into a business-friendly narrative.
instructions: Report ROI %, wasted-license cost, and the expansion probability with its recommendation.
Reconcile the model ROI with the first-principles finance_model and flag any material gap.
Treat outputs as directional because the models are trained on synthetic data.

functions.name: render_value_card
functions.description: Render a standardized Copilot ROI Adaptive Card from either tool outputs or prompt-based reasoning outputs.

roi_percent_numeric.description: Numeric ROI for automatic health classification when needed.

model_roi_percent.description: Model ROI text shown in the caption.

roi_caption.description: Short label indicating the ROI basis.

health.description: Health label, for example Strong, Moderate, or At Risk.

health_color.description: Adaptive Card color token (Good, Warning, Attention, Default).

expansion_color.description: Adaptive Card color token for the expansion row.

returns.description: A JSON payload containing a card object compatible with the standard ROI Adaptive Card template.

reasoning.description: Use this function to standardize final output rendering.
instructions: Call this function for final answer rendering even when numbers were computed directly in prompt reasoning.
When a field is unavailable, pass a concise placeholder instead of omitting the card.

responding.description: Return a card-shaped payload for consistent user experience.
instructions: Keep the narrative concise and decision-oriented.
Use this as the default final render path for ROI responses.

functions.name: benchmark_department_value
functions.description: Benchmark departments by ROI drivers, rank low performers, and return targeted Copilot recommendations with a comparison chart URL.

company_name.description: Optional company label for the benchmark output.

departments_json.description: JSON stringified array of department rows. Each row should include: name, licensed_users, active_users, roi_percent_month, avg_hours_saved_per_user_month, and optional adoption_rate, waste_cost_month_usd.

low_performer_count.description: How many low-performing departments to highlight. Defaults to 3.

returns.description: A JSON payload containing ranked departments, low-performer recommendations, and a chart URL.

reasoning.description: Use this function when department-level data is available and comparative prioritization is needed.
instructions: Rank departments by intervention opportunity using adoption, ROI, hours saved, and waste indicators.
Highlight low performers and return concrete department-specific actions.

responding.description: Return a benchmark summary suitable for Adaptive Card rendering and narrative follow-up.
